# The Mander Tracker

> The Mander Tracker is an interactive tracker of US congressional
> redistricting and mid-decade gerrymandering across the 2024-2026 cycle.
> It shows before-and-after district maps for every state that has
> redrawn (or tried to redraw) its boundaries, plus a deep-linked news
> timeline of the litigation and legislation that drove the changes.
> Data is verified through 2026-05-13.

The site is a single page that combines:

- An interactive US map color-coded by redistricting status (passed,
  planned, failed, no action) and which party benefited.
- A per-state detail view with side-by-side before/after district maps
  drawn from the same source as Jeffrey B. Lewis's congressional
  district GIS collection.
- A filterable, sourced news feed of the cycle's key events — every
  source URL is a deep link (SCOTUS opinion PDF, secretary-of-state
  results page, legislative bill page, or specific article).

## Coverage

- [Full timeline + structured state data](https://mandertracker.com/llms-full.txt): the
  same headlines, descriptions, dates, and source URLs that appear in
  the news feed and state cards, in a single readable Markdown file
  optimised for LLM ingestion.
- [Interactive map](https://mandertracker.com/): the canonical view.

## Methodology

- States tracked: NC, NY, TX, CA, AL, LA, GA, OH, FL, TN, MO, UT, IL,
  IN, KS, MD, NM, SC, VA, WI — 20 states with material activity in the
  current cycle (redraws, court rulings, or failed efforts).
- Status taxonomy: *passed* (new map in effect), *planned* (announced
  or in legal limbo), *failed* (effort blocked or challenge defeated),
  *none* (no action).
- Per-district party leans are stylized for the chart only — they are
  not Cook PVI or a rolled-up presidential-result projection. Aggregate
  seat counts (before/after) match published projections.
- Geometry source: Jeffrey B. Lewis's congressional district boundaries
  repository (cutoff 119th Congress, 2024 election). Boundaries for the
  most recent mid-decade redraws (post-Aug 2025) may pre-date the latest
  enacted map; the schematic district arrays carry the post-redraw
  partisan picture.

## Stack

Static SPA built with Vite + React + Tailwind, deployed on Cloudflare
Workers static assets. No tracking, no analytics, no auth.
